{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resource Officer",
  "description": "Reliable Plastic was incorporated on April 28th, 1998. We have been committed to providing the best quality products to our customers since its inception. Apart from the outstanding service that is provided, consumers are sure to be completely satisfied with the end product. Reliable Plastic promises to use only quality material for your product. Everything is done in-house with the company and therefore is your one-stop shop for product manufacturing. The difference is our service! We stand behind our products and provide the best, most comprehensive ongoing customer service. We take the time to learn about your needs and work with you to provide the best fastening solutions for your specific application and welcome orders requiring our custom fabrication services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Officer

Job Location: Elele, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Agriculture/Farming
Reports To: Farm Manager

Job Summary


	We are seeking an experienced and proactive Human Resource Officer to oversee and coordinate all HR functions within our agricultural farming operations.
	The ideal candidate must possess strong knowledge of human resource management practices, labor laws, workforce administration, employee relations, and farm workforce management.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for ensuring efficient HR operations while supporting the organization&#39;s productivity and growth objectives.


Key Responsibilities
Recruitment and Staffing:


	Coordinate end-to-end recruitment processes for farm workers, supervisors, technical staff, and administrative employees.
	Conduct interviews, employee onboarding, orientation, and induction programs.
	Develop manpower plans based on farm operational requirements.
	Maintain a talent pipeline for seasonal and permanent workforce needs.


Employee Relations and Welfare:


	Serve as the primary contact for employee grievances and conflict resolution.
	Foster positive employee relations and workplace harmony.
	Coordinate employee welfare programs, accommodation issues, transportation, and other welfare initiatives where applicable.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and labor regulations.


Performance Management:


	Monitor employee performance and attendance.
	Coordinate performance appraisal exercises and probation reviews.
	Provide support to department heads on employee performance improvement plans.
	Recommend disciplinary actions where necessary.


Payroll and Compensation Administration:


	Prepare and maintain accurate employee records for payroll processing.
	Monitor attendance, overtime, leave records, and shift schedules.
	Support salary administration and benefits management.
	Ensure payroll data accuracy and compliance.


Training and Development:


	Identify training needs across farm operations.
	Organize employee training programs on safety, compliance, productivity, and workplace conduct.
	Monitor training effectiveness and employee development initiatives.


Compliance and Industrial Relations:


	Ensure compliance with Nigerian Labour Laws and company policies.
	Maintain proper HR documentation and statutory records.
	Handle disciplinary matters, investigations, warnings, and employee exits.
	Ensure workplace health, safety, and environmental compliance from an HR perspective.


HR Administration:


	Maintain employee files and HR databases.
	Generate periodic HR reports and workforce analytics.
	Manage leave administration and employee documentation.
	Conduct exit interviews and employee clearance processes.


Job Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or HND in Human Resource Management, Business Administration, Industrial Relations, Psychology, Sociology, or a related field.


Experience:


	Minimum of 3&ndash;7 years of proven experience in Human Resource Management.
	Must have practical HR experience within an Agricultural Farm, Agro-Allied Company, Plantation, Livestock Farm, Fish Farm, Poultry Farm, or related agricultural environment.
	Experience managing both permanent and casual workforce is highly desirable.


Knowledge and Skills:


	Comprehensive understanding of all Human Resource functions and operations.
	Strong knowledge of recruitment, onboarding, employee relations, payroll administration, performance management, and disciplinary procedures.
	Good understanding of Nigerian Labour Laws and employment regulations.
	Experience handling large farm workforce operations.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and conflict resolution skills.
	Strong organizational and record-keeping abilities.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).
	Ability to work independently and under minimal supervision.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.


Competencies:


	Leadership and interpersonal skills.
	High level of integrity and confidentiality.
	Attention to detail.
	Time management and multitasking ability.
	Decision-making and sound judgment.
	Ability to thrive in a fast-paced agricultural environment.


Additional Requirements:


	Must be willing to relocate if necessary.
	Must be comfortable working in a farm environment.
	Ability to manage both field workers and administrative staff effectively.
	Professional HR certifications such as CIPM, SHRM, HRCI, or related certifications will be an added advantage.


Expected Deliverables:


	Effective workforce planning and manpower management.
	Improved employee productivity and engagement.
	Compliance with labor laws and company policies.
	Timely recruitment and onboarding of employees.
	Accurate payroll and personnel administration.
	Reduced employee relations issues and improved workplace discipline.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV / Application to: reliablesteelplasticrecruit@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-03",
  "validThrough": "2026-07-02",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "RELIABLE STEEL AND PLASTIC IND. LTD"
    
  },

      "@type": "PostalAddress",

      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },

      "maxValue": 0,

    }

}



Human Resource Officer Job at Reliable Steel and Plastic Limited




        
    

        
        Reliable Plastic was incorporated on April 28th, 1998. We have been committed to providing the best quality products to our customers since its inception. Apart from the outstanding service that is provided, consumers are sure to be completely satisfied with the end product. Reliable Plastic promises to use only quality material for your product. Everything is done in-house with the company and therefore is your one-stop shop for product manufacturing. The difference is our service! We stand behind our products and provide the best, most comprehensive ongoing customer service. We take the time to learn about your needs and work with you to provide the best fastening solutions for your specific application and welcome orders requiring our custom fabrication services.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Officer

Job Location: Elele, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Agriculture/Farming
Reports To: Farm Manager

Job Summary


	We are seeking an experienced and proactive Human Resource Officer to oversee and coordinate all HR functions within our agricultural farming operations.
	The ideal candidate must possess strong knowledge of human resource management practices, labor laws, workforce administration, employee relations, and farm workforce management.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for ensuring efficient HR operations while supporting the organization's productivity and growth objectives.


Key Responsibilities
Recruitment and Staffing:


	Coordinate end-to-end recruitment processes for farm workers, supervisors, technical staff, and administrative employees.
	Conduct interviews, employee onboarding, orientation, and induction programs.
	Develop manpower plans based on farm operational requirements.
	Maintain a talent pipeline for seasonal and permanent workforce needs.


Employee Relations and Welfare:


	Serve as the primary contact for employee grievances and conflict resolution.
	Foster positive employee relations and workplace harmony.
	Coordinate employee welfare programs, accommodation issues, transportation, and other welfare initiatives where applicable.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and labor regulations.


Performance Management:


	Monitor employee performance and attendance.
	Coordinate performance appraisal exercises and probation reviews.
	Provide support to department heads on employee performance improvement plans.
	Recommend disciplinary actions where necessary.


Payroll and Compensation Administration:


	Prepare and maintain accurate employee records for payroll processing.
	Monitor attendance, overtime, leave records, and shift schedules.
	Support salary administration and benefits management.
	Ensure payroll data accuracy and compliance.


Training and Development:


	Identify training needs across farm operations.
	Organize employee training programs on safety, compliance, productivity, and workplace conduct.
	Monitor training effectiveness and employee development initiatives.


Compliance and Industrial Relations:


	Ensure compliance with Nigerian Labour Laws and company policies.
	Maintain proper HR documentation and statutory records.
	Handle disciplinary matters, investigations, warnings, and employee exits.
	Ensure workplace health, safety, and environmental compliance from an HR perspective.


HR Administration:


	Maintain employee files and HR databases.
	Generate periodic HR reports and workforce analytics.
	Manage leave administration and employee documentation.
	Conduct exit interviews and employee clearance processes.


Job Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor's Degree or HND in Human Resource Management, Business Administration, Industrial Relations, Psychology, Sociology, or a related field.


Experience:


	Minimum of 3–7 years of proven experience in Human Resource Management.
	Must have practical HR experience within an Agricultural Farm, Agro-Allied Company, Plantation, Livestock Farm, Fish Farm, Poultry Farm, or related agricultural environment.
	Experience managing both permanent and casual workforce is highly desirable.


Knowledge and Skills:


	Comprehensive understanding of all Human Resource functions and operations.
	Strong knowledge of recruitment, onboarding, employee relations, payroll administration, performance management, and disciplinary procedures.
	Good understanding of Nigerian Labour Laws and employment regulations.
	Experience handling large farm workforce operations.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and conflict resolution skills.
	Strong organizational and record-keeping abilities.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).
	Ability to work independently and under minimal supervision.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.


Competencies:


	Leadership and interpersonal skills.
	High level of integrity and confidentiality.
	Attention to detail.
	Time management and multitasking ability.
	Decision-making and sound judgment.
	Ability to thrive in a fast-paced agricultural environment.


Additional Requirements:


	Must be willing to relocate if necessary.
	Must be comfortable working in a farm environment.
	Ability to manage both field workers and administrative staff effectively.
	Professional HR certifications such as CIPM, SHRM, HRCI, or related certifications will be an added advantage.


Expected Deliverables:


	Effective workforce planning and manpower management.
	Improved employee productivity and engagement.
	Compliance with labor laws and company policies.
	Timely recruitment and onboarding of employees.
	Accurate payroll and personnel administration.
	Reduced employee relations issues and improved workplace discipline.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV / Application to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

title: Job Openings at Staredge Public Health Concept
content:
Staredge Public Health Concept is a consultancy firm concerned withEnvironmental Sanitation, Pest & Vector Control, System Development & Management, Capacity Building & System, Strengthening for food safety & environmental Health Response, Environmental Hazard Prevention & Control, Environmental impact assessment, environmental auditing, Restoration work, Renovation, Energy, Engineering Installations, Urban structure, Events Management, engineering and architecture, landscape infrastructure, hydraulics & environment, telecoms trading, sales and distribution of general goods & general contractors.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Admin / Finance Officer
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Garki, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Handle day-to-day admin: scheduling, filing, correspondence, office supplies
	Process invoices, payments, receipts + maintain financial records
	Support payroll preparation and bank reconciliations
	Track expenses, budgets, and prepare monthly reports for management
	Liaise with banks, vendors, and auditors when needed
	Any other admin/finance duties as assigned


Requirements


	Minimum HND / B.Sc Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Admin, or related fields
	1-2 years of experience in admin + finance roles preferred
	Strong Excel/Google Sheets skills. QuickBooks/Sage is a plus
	High integrity, confidentiality, and attention to detail
	Good communication + Microsoft Office proficiency.


Application Closing Date: 26th July, 2026. 

Click here to apply online

 

 

2.) Customer Relationship Officer

Job Location: Abuja
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities


	Contact clients and establishments through phone calls and physical visits.
	Confirm compliance status of establishments.
	Encourage and follow up with non-compliant establishments to ensure compliance.
	Maintain proper records of all client interactions and follow-up activities.
	Meet assigned dailytargets.
	Prepare and submit routine reports.


Work structure


	Monday to Friday
	Officers will be assigned a company vehicle and driver for official duties
	Daily operational targets must be achieved


Requirements


	Minimum of HND/BSc or equivalent
	Strong communication, persuasion, and interpersonal skills
	Ability to meet daily targets consistently
	Good record-keeping and reporting ability
	Prior customer service, field, or compliance experience is an added advantage.


Salary
N150,000 per month (Attractive and negotiable based on experience). 

Application Closing Date: 16th July, 2026. 

Click here to apply online

 

3.) Zonal Manager

Job Location: Bwari Area Council, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are currently seeking a competent, organized, and result-driven professional to serve as Zonal Manager for our Kubwa & Dutse Office operations.
	The successful candidate will oversee daily administrative, operational, and environmental management activities within the zone and ensure smooth coordination of all office and field operations.


Job Responsibilities


	Coordinate waste management and other environmental/public health activities within the zone
	Supervise office operations and ensure efficient workflow
	Manage office documents, records, and resources effectively
	Oversee the maintenance and proper use of all office vehicles
	Ensure smooth and timely dispatch of notices and field operations
	Coordinate field officers and monitor operational performance
	Prepare reports and attend weekly operational review meetings


Requirements


	Minimum of HND/BSc in Administration, Environmental Management, Public Health, or related field
	Strong leadership and organizational skills
	Ability to coordinate teams and manage operations effectively
	Good communication and reporting skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications
	Prior management or field operations experience is an added advantage


Work Schedule:


	Monday to Friday
	Weekly review meetings every Saturday.


Salary
N150,000 monthly.

Application Closing Date: 15th June, 2026.

Click here to apply online    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Accountant",
  "description": "Neveah Limited is a leading commodity trading company established in 2014. The company specializes in the export of solid minerals and agricultural products, sourcing the finest raw materials from local markets to meet international demand. Neveah has now expanded into manufacturing, focusing on the recycling of aluminum and copper to produce ingots for export, further strengthening its global footprint.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location: Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Brief Job Overview


	We are looking for a Accountant to join our team, a mid-level experienced candidate who will support the Finance / Accounts Unit with the task of guiding businesses and individuals in decisions about spending money to attain profit.
	You will analyse data, evaluate investment opportunities, and provide recommendations to stakeholders / Firms.


Responsibilities
The ideal candidate will (but not limited to):


	Ensures general ledger, subsidiary ledgers, and other financial records and schedules are accurately maintained.
	Institutes policies and procedures capable of protecting the business resources against waste, fraud, and inefficiency.
	Maintains procedures to establish tight control over cash transactions.
	Ensures timely follow up of accounts receivable and payable.
	Evaluates &amp; implements internal and external audit recommendations &amp; provides feedback to management on results.
	Implements budget and forecast preparation schedule by collecting relevant business data and projections from the line departments and provide relevant analyses and feedback.
	Provides support, service and advice in all finance matters to the company&rsquo;s management.
	Exercises budgetary controls, accounting &amp; physical controls and credit controls as per laid down procedures and asset management.
	Maintains an effective involvement and understanding of the business to contribute to key business decisions within the Company through financial advice and expertise.
	Provide creative alternatives and recommendations to reduce costs and improve financial performance.
	Identify trends, advise the company and recommend actions to a senior financial analyst based on sound analysis
	Gain and update job knowledge to remain informed about novelty in the field
	Consult with management to guide and influence long-term and strategic decision-making within the broadest scope
	Drive process improvement and policy development initiatives that impact the function
	Other duties as may be assigned.


Qualifications


	B.Sc Degree in Finance, Economics or a related field.
	A qualified chartered accountant with 3 - 5 years of experience in a similar role within the manufacturing/export Industry.
	Proven working experience as a Finance Analyst.
	Proficient in spreadsheets, databases, MS Office and financial software applications.
	Hands-on experience with statistical analysis and statistical packages.
	Outstanding presentation, reporting and communication skills.
	Proven knowledge of financial forecasting and diagnosis, corporate finance and information analysis.
	Well informed in current financial subjects, accounting, tax laws, money market and business environments.
	ICAN/ ACCA Professional Membership is required..
	Must be able to use modern accounting software.


Required Competencies:


	Technical Accounting Skills.
	Mastery of Accounting Principles.
	Proven work experience as a Finance Analyst or similar role.
	Solid knowledge of relevant regulatory standards.
	Good communication skills, both verbal and written.
	Excellent data collection and analysis skills.
	Strong attention to detail
	Relevant training and certifications.
	Multi-tasking skills, with the ability to prioritize tasks.


Salary
N299,999 - N350,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: careers@neveah.com.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Accountant Job at Neveah Limited




        
    

        
        Neveah Limited is a leading commodity trading company established in 2014. The company specializes in the export of solid minerals and agricultural products, sourcing the finest raw materials from local markets to meet international demand. Neveah has now expanded into manufacturing, focusing on the recycling of aluminum and copper to produce ingots for export, further strengthening its global footprint.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location: Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Brief Job Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a Accountant to join our team, a mid-level experienced candidate who will support the Finance / Accounts Unit with the task of guiding businesses and individuals in decisions about spending money to attain profit.
	You will analyse data, evaluate investment opportunities, and provide recommendations to stakeholders / Firms.


Responsibilities
The ideal candidate will (but not limited to):


	Ensures general ledger, subsidiary ledgers, and other financial records and schedules are accurately maintained.
	Institutes policies and procedures capable of protecting the business resources against waste, fraud, and inefficiency.
	Maintains procedures to establish tight control over cash transactions.
	Ensures timely follow up of accounts receivable and payable.
	Evaluates & implements internal and external audit recommendations & provides feedback to management on results.
	Implements budget and forecast preparation schedule by collecting relevant business data and projections from the line departments and provide relevant analyses and feedback.
	Provides support, service and advice in all finance matters to the company’s management.
	Exercises budgetary controls, accounting & physical controls and credit controls as per laid down procedures and asset management.
	Maintains an effective involvement and understanding of the business to contribute to key business decisions within the Company through financial advice and expertise.
	Provide creative alternatives and recommendations to reduce costs and improve financial performance.
	Identify trends, advise the company and recommend actions to a senior financial analyst based on sound analysis
	Gain and update job knowledge to remain informed about novelty in the field
	Consult with management to guide and influence long-term and strategic decision-making within the broadest scope
	Drive process improvement and policy development initiatives that impact the function
	Other duties as may be assigned.


Qualifications


	B.Sc Degree in Finance, Economics or a related field.
	A qualified chartered accountant with 3 - 5 years of experience in a similar role within the manufacturing/export Industry.
	Proven working experience as a Finance Analyst.
	Proficient in spreadsheets, databases, MS Office and financial software applications.
	Hands-on experience with statistical analysis and statistical packages.
	Outstanding presentation, reporting and communication skills.
	Proven knowledge of financial forecasting and diagnosis, corporate finance and information analysis.
	Well informed in current financial subjects, accounting, tax laws, money market and business environments.
	ICAN/ ACCA Professional Membership is required..
	Must be able to use modern accounting software.


Required Competencies:


	Technical Accounting Skills.
	Mastery of Accounting Principles.
	Proven work experience as a Finance Analyst or similar role.
	Solid knowledge of relevant regulatory standards.
	Good communication skills, both verbal and written.
	Excellent data collection and analysis skills.
	Strong attention to detail
	Relevant training and certifications.
	Multi-tasking skills, with the ability to prioritize tasks.


Salary
N299,999 - N350,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at SYOJ Consulting and Management Services
content:
SYOJ Consulting and Management Services otherwise know as SYOJ Consulting is a company deeply commiteed to delivering leading edge HR Consultancy and Management Service to our client.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Senior Lawyer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Purpose / Objectives
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a highly motivated, strategic, and results-driven professional to lead our Legal Team.
	As a Senior Lawyer, you will be responsible for providing sound legal advisory services, manage litigation and corporate legal matters, ensure compliance with applicable laws and regulations, protect the organization’s legal interests, and support management in achieving strategic business objectives.


Responsibilities


	Provide legal advice and guidance on corporate, commercial, regulatory, and operational matters.
	Draft, review, negotiate, and vet contracts, agreements, memoranda, and other legal documents.
	Represent the organization in litigation, arbitration, mediation, and other dispute resolution proceedings.
	Ensure compliance with statutory, regulatory, and corporate governance requirements.
	Manage external solicitors, consultants, and regulatory relationships where necessary.
	Conduct legal research and provide opinions on emerging legal and regulatory issues.
	Prepare and review legal correspondence, reports, and documentation.
	Advise management on legal risks and recommend appropriate mitigation measures.
	Handle employment and labour-related legal matters when required.
	Support company secretarial functions including board resolutions, statutory filings, and corporate documentation.
	Monitor changes in legislation and ensure organizational compliance with applicable laws.
	Participate in policy formulation and review to ensure legal adequacy.


Requirements


	Bachelor of Laws (LL.B) and Barrister-at-Law (B.L) qualification.
	Minimum of 10 years of post-call experience in legal practice or corporate legal advisory services.
	Membership of the Nigerian Bar Association (NBA) and possession of a valid practicing license.
	Strong knowledge of corporate, commercial, labour, and regulatory laws.
	Excellent advocacy, negotiation, drafting, and analytical skills.
	Ability to manage complex legal matters with minimal supervision.
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	High level of integrity, professionalism, and confidentiality.
	Proficiency in legal research and use of Microsoft Office applications.
	Experience in litigation and dispute resolution will be an added advantage.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

2.) Dining and Event Service Coordinator

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Purpose / Objectives


	We are seeking a highly motivated, strategic, and results-driven professional to join our Operations / Food & Beverage Services team.
	As Dining & Event Service Coordinator, you will be responsible for the effective coordination, supervision, and control of all dining and beverage service operations during Club events and functions.


The primary purpose of the role is to ensure:


	Efficient coordination between the kitchen, bar, and dining hall operations.
	Proper deployment and supervision of stewards and service staff
	Prompt, professional, and courteous service delivery to members and guests.
	A consistently pleasant, seamless, and high-quality dining experience during all Club events and functions.
	The role is operational and service-focused and is primarily concerned with:
	Dining hall service execution
	Steward management and discipline
	Guest satisfaction and service standards
	Event dining coordination


The role does not include responsibility for:


	Event sales
	Reservations or bookings
	Marketing or business development activities


Responsibilities


	Dining Service Coordination
	Steward Management and Supervision
	Guest Dining Experience Management
	Kitchen and Bar Coordination
	Event Operations and Venue Oversight
	Reporting and Operational Control


Authority and Accountability
The Dining & Event Service Coordinator shall:


	Exercise operational supervision over stewards and assigned service personnel during events.
	Issue operational instructions relating to dining service execution and guest handling during functions.


Be accountable for:


	Dining service coordination, Steward effectiveness, Dining hall operational efficiency, guests’ dining experience during events.


The role shall not:


	Approve financial expenditures without authorization
	Recruit or dismiss staff independently
	Override Management policies or operational directives.


Requirements


	Candidates should possess a B.Sc Degree / HND qualification with 5 - 10 years of work experience.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

3.) Head of Office Operation - Facilities Management / Estate Management

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Purpose/ Objectives


	We are seeking a highly motivated, strategic, and results-driven professional to lead our Facilities/Estate Mgt team. As the Head of Office Operation - Facilities Management / Estate Management, you will be responsible for strategic planning and efficient management of all office facilities, estates, and infrastructure to ensure a safe, cost-effective, and high-performing work environment.


Responsibilities


	Strategic Leadership: Drives facilities and estate strategies, including space planning, expansion, and sustainability initiatives.
	Facilities Management: Oversees maintenance, operations, and optimal functionality of all buildings and infrastructure.
	Vendor Management: Manages service providers, contracts, and SLAs to ensure quality and cost efficiency.
	Health & Safety: Ensures full compliance with regulatory, health, and safety standards across all locations.
	Budget Control: Manages budgets, monitors costs, and drives operational efficiency and savings.
	Project Management: Leads construction, renovation, and office fit-out projects end-toend.
	Office Operations: Ensures smooth daily office operations and enhances workplace experience.
	 Leadership: Leads, develops, and holds the facilities team accountable for performance delivery.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in estate management, Facilities Management, Engineering, or related field.
	Minimum of 10–15 years of experience in facilities, estate, or property management. (Compulsory)
	At least 5 years in a senior leadership or managerial role.
	Master’s degree (MBA or relevant discipline) is an added advantage.
	Relevant certifications such as IFMA, RICS, NIESV, COREN, NSE or equivalent are highly desirable.
	Proven experience managing multi-site operations and corporate office environments.
	Strong track record in project management (construction, renovation, fit outs).
	Strong knowledge of facility systems (HVAC, electrical, plumbing, building maintenance).
	Solid understanding of health, safety, and regulatory compliance.
	Strong leadership, strategic thinking, and execution ability.
	Excellent decision-making, stakeholder management, and operational improvement capability.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
